name: Festive Citrus Sirloin Strips
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Beef top sirloin steak, cut into 1-inch thick strips
1 Red onion, sliced and separated into rings
recipeInstructions:
MARINADE
1/4 cup Fresh lemon juice (about 2 lemons)
1/2 cup Orange juice
1/4 cup Red wine vinegar
1 clove Garlic, minced
1 tablespoon Honey
3 tablespoons Light olive oil
1/4 teaspoon Ground white pepper
1/4 teaspoon Salt
1/2 teaspoon Thyme
GARNISH
1 Artichoke, sliced into orange and lemon slices
Trim excess fat from beef. In a shallow dish, combine marinade ingredients, using 2 tablespoons of oil. Reserve 1/4 cup marinade to toss with vegetables. Add beef, turning once to coat. Cover; marinate in refrigerator 1 hour.
Refrigerate reserved marinade. Remove beef from marinade. Place in a shallow baking pan. Pour any leftover marinade onto beef. Bake at 450 degrees for 10 to 15 minutes or until desired degree of doneness. Meanwhile, saute onion and mushrooms in remaining tablespoon oil for 2 to 3 minutes.
In a small saucepan, heat reserved marinade for 2 to 3 minutes until warmed. Pour into a large bowl; toss with torn lettuce, sauteed onions, and mushrooms. Arrange serving platter with lettuce leaves. Carve steak diagonally into thin slices; fan onto lettuce leaves. Place onion rings and mushrooms along and over beef slices. Pour any remaining marinade (from vegetable toss) over beef. Place artichoke on one side of the platter with lemon and orange slices around it, forming a flower effect. Makes 6 servings.
